title: Modified moirÃ© lens
abstract: The present invention relates to an optical system comprising a phase modulating element, and a light reflecting element.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. An optical system comprising: a phase modulating element wherein the phase modulating element has a phase function F(G RM , Q RM ) expressed in polar coordinates which is an odd function with regard to the polar angle; a light reflecting element defining an axis, wherein light impinging upon the light reflecting element parallel to said axis at any point with polar coordinate 0LR is reflected from the light reflecting element parallel to said axis with the polar coordinate -0LR; a mount for supporting the phase modulating element and/or the light reflecting element, the mount being configured to enable a relative polar rotation between the phase modulating element and the light reflecting element; and an optical arrangement interposed between the phase modulating element and the light reflecting element; wherein the phase modulating element, the optical arrangement and the light reflecting element are arranged and configured such that light interacting with the phase modulating element and passing through the optical arrangement is reflected by the light reflecting element back through the optical arrangement onto the phase modulating element. 2. The optical system of claim 1, wherein the optical arrangement comprises a number of focusing elements N, preferably wherein N is equal to any of 1, 2, or 3; wherein a distance between the phase modulating element and the first focusing element along the optical axis is equal to the focal length of the first focusing element. 3 The optical system of any one of the previous claims, wherein the phase modulating element is one or a combination of a diffractive optical element, a refractive element, a meta lens, a holographic optical element or a gradient index optics element. 4. The optical system of any one of the previous claims, wherein the phase modulating element is light transmissive. 5. The optical system of any one of claims 1-3, wherein the phase modulating element is light reflective. 6. The optical system of any one of the previous claims, further comprising: a half-wave plate; and/or a polarizing or non-polarizing beam splitter. 7. The optical system of claim 6, wherein the optical system is configured to direct light through the half-wave plate and the beam splitter before interacting with the phase modulating element and wherein the optical system is configured to emit light from the beam splitter into a different direction than the incident light is coming from, after reflecting off of the light reflecting element. 8. The optical system of claim 6 or claim 7 wherein the half-wave plate, the beam splitter and the phase modulating element are positioned along a first optical axis; and wherein the phase modulating element, the optical arrangement, and the light reflecting element are positioned along a second optical axis. 9. The optical system of claim 8, wherein the first optical axis and the second optical axis are aligned. 10. The optical system of claim 8, wherein the first optical axis and the second optical axis form an angle of between 1Â° and 179Â°, preferably wherein the angle is between 1Â° and 90Â°, more preferably wherein the angle is between 1Â° and 60Â°. 11. The optical system of any one of the previous claims, wherein the mount comprises a actuator or motor for enabling the relative polar rotation between the phase modulating element and the light reflecting element, preferably wherein the actuator or motor comprises a galvo scanner, a stepper motor, a voice coil motor, a piezo motor, a DC motor, a servo motor, and/or a MEMS based micro motor. 12. The optical system of any one of the previous claims, wherein the phase modulating element, the optical arrangement and the light reflecting element are positioned such that the light initially modulated by the phase modulating element is modulated with a modulation function 

Ti, and after being reflected from the light reflecting element, the light is subsequently modulated by the same phase modulating element with a modulation function T2 such that the overall modulation function T combi (0 rei )= Ti*T2 corresponds to an optically combined phase modulating optical element and depends on the relative polar rotation angle. 13. The optical system of any one of the previous claims, wherein the relative polar rotation angle between the phase modulating element and the light reflecting element alters the focal length, and/or the beam mode and/or the resulting global phase of the optical system. 14. The optical system of any one of the previous claims, wherein the phase modulating element has a circular effective area, preferably the effective area has a diameter A of 50 mm or less, preferably 25 mm or less, more preferably 10 mm or less. 15. The optical system of claim 14, wherein the focal length of the optical system may be altered between -100*A and +100*A preferably between -10* A and +10* A, more preferably between -1*A and 1*A.
